ks1.objective: Dribble your football across the area like a kart on the track — avoid the obstacles!
successCriteria: Keeps the ball close while moving
Picks a character and stays in role

lks2.objective: Dribble at speed across the area while reacting to layered obstacles — speed, agility and ball control.
successCriteria: Dribbles at top speed when clear
Stays calm when caught by a 'banana skin'

equipment.qty: 1 per child + extras for banana skins

space.description: Long playing area set up as a race track. Start without obstacles — add them as the session progresses. All children need a ball each at the start.

steps: Children line up at one end of the area, each with their own ball.
Task: dribble the ball to the other side.
Progress by adding different obstacles, making it harder to cross.
Encourage children to pick a Mario Kart character at the start — refer to characters throughout.
Run several rounds, adding one obstacle each round.
progressions: Banana Skins (bibs!): If a child's ball hits a bib on the floor, they pretend to be dazed and spin around twice before continuing.
Shells (cones): If a child's ball hits a cone, they must pause, perform 5 toe taps, and then continue.
Giant Venus Fly Traps (hoops): If a child dribbles into a hoop, they stay inside and wait for a high five from another child before continuing.
Add a countdown — if children don't cross the finish line in time, they lose a life.
questions: Which power-up was the hardest to avoid?
How did you change your speed when you saw an obstacle?
Which character were you and why?

step.space: Change the width of the area to make it more congested and harder
step.task: Encourage children to dream up additional Mario Kart-style challenges
step.equipment: Add additional equipment and obstacles — tap into children's imagination
step.people: Send children across in waves, then everyone at once

base.showmanship: Get children to pick a different character at the start of the session. Reference their character throughout — "Mario, watch out for that banana!" — adds an extra layer of fun. Parents love hearing creativity and character work alongside the football.
